prompt: Vertical portrait 9:16 high-resolution atlas world map for a Warhammer 40,000 Dark Imperium inspired grimdark science-fantasy RPG setting. Terrain type: continent_archipelago. Draw one dominant fictional planetary continent plus a large irregular offshore archipelago as an Imperial cartographic war-zone atlas with gothic sci-fi architecture, mechanicus technical overlay, and toxic ochre atmosphere. The mainland should include hive city mega-structures, underhive slums, industrial wastelands, toxic promethium refineries, ruined manufactorum zones, void station orbital platform anchors, cathedral-fortress spires, inquisitorial quarantine sectors, trench lines, irradiated deserts, frozen wastes, polluted deltas, acidic marshes, and xenos-contaminated badlands. The archipelago should be uneven and varied: refinery islands, fortress-monastery islands, quarantine isles, wrecked void-port rocks, toxic shoals, sea-wall defense platforms, tiny minefield reefs, and broad industrial island provinces. Avoid a simple vertical split; make the continent-and-archipelago separation curve in a diagonal / C-shaped polluted gulf with twisting straits, bays, shoals, wreck channels, and broken coastal shelves. Coastlines must be realistic and complex with peninsulas, bays, gulfs, straits, broken shores, curved coasts, river deltas, fjords, capes, lagoons, barrier islands, sea caves, reefs, and shoals. Large detailed grimdark planetary atlas, tactical grid and gothic ornaments allowed, no readable labels, no legible text, no logos, no modern satellite style.
